item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one! I feel like at the end we're supposed to assume that the figures did something bad to him (?) but i prefer to read it as the story of a bunch of strange beings (maybe sharing a collective conscious) that just really liked Elvis, and are trying to preserve his memory :( Is there a "correct" way to interpret these? Who knows.

Anyway, love the concept in general of an elvis impersonator SCP. Or eldritch and creepily obsessed Elvis fans or whatever this is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interviews and log at the end are very evocative but the description itself is very lackluster. The repetition of phrasing in “SCP-4254 will do this/as a result so will this” makes it feel very railroaded. I think not enough attention is given to the manifestation events, I think of you had these pieces of Elvis out here living lives in Las Vegas trailer parks living as impersonators it would lend them more depth. Instead they’re just narrative vehicles which ends up making the primary anomalous effect an almost superfluous part of its own article.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to AtlasIncarnate, Dave, Ellie, A man tortured by horses, Wolfsblood and superorangejuice for their feedback!

PLEASE DON’T SUE TANHONY
